summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a crafted metadata value injects additional ExifTool arguments and causes the target container to create `/home/gotenberg/evohunt-proof.pdf` outside the request working directory.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should make `POST /forms/pdfengines/metadata/write` return HTTP 404 because the expected output file was moved away, while the target container gains `/home/gotenberg/evohunt-proof.pdf` with attacker-chosen path components and metadata.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned the bundled vulnerable source snapshot, rebuilt the target image, sent the crafted metadata request, observed the expected HTTP 404 response, and verified inside the target container that `/home/gotenberg/evohunt-proof.pdf` existed with `Title : poc`, `Directory : /home/gotenberg`, and `File Name : evohunt-proof.pdf`.
verification_method: The run script first confirms the proof file is absent, then uses the helper image to submit the crafted multipart request and assert the HTTP 404 / `Not Found` response. After that it uses `docker exec` against the target container to confirm file existence and to query the created PDF with `exiftool`, proving that injected `Directory` and `FileName` arguments controlled the final output path.
evidence_produced_by_run: The helper request observed the expected HTTP 404 response from `POST /forms/pdfengines/metadata/write` after the output file was moved out of the request directory.
The target container contained `/home/gotenberg/evohunt-proof.pdf` after the request even though that path did not exist before exploitation.
Inspecting the created file inside the container reported `Title : poc`, `Directory : /home/gotenberg`, and `File Name : evohunt-proof.pdf`.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the bundled bare target-repository snapshot on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, rebuilds the target image from that checkout, and then exercises the vulnerability against the rebuilt container.
